title: Magnetizing system for needle assemblies
abstract: A magnetizing system for use in selectively magnetizing a needle of a medical device is disclosed. Once magnetized, the needle can be tracked by a needle guidance system, which assists the clinician placing the needle by visualizing on a display the position and orientation of the needle after its insertion into a body of a patient. An orientation key system is included with the magnetizing system to ensure the needle is positioned correctly in the magnetizing system. This in turn ensures that the needle is properly magnetized so as to be accurately tracked by the needle guidance system. In one embodiment a first key is included with the magnetizing system and configured to cooperatively mate with a second key included with the needle of the medical device. Various types of keys are disclosed herein.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A guidance system for guiding insertion of a needle into a body of a patient, comprising: an ultrasound imaging device including an ultrasound probe; a magnetic sensor configured to sense a magnetic field associated with the needle; a processor that receives magnetic field data sensed by the magnetic sensor to determine a position of the needle in three spatial dimensions; and a magnetizer configured to magnetize the needle, the magnetizer including an orientation key system configured to position the needle in a desired orientation with respect to the magnetizer. 2. The system as defined in claim 1, further comprising a display that depicts the determined position of the needle together with an image of an internal portion of the body of the patient by the ultrasound imaging device. 3. The system as defined in claim 1, wherein the orientation key system includes a first key feature included with the magnetizer and a second key feature associated with the needle. 4. The system as defined in claim 3, wherein the second key feature is included on a needle cover that is removably attachable to the needle. 5. The system as defined in claim 1, wherein the magnetizer includes a body, the body including a magnetic component configured to magnetize the needle. 6. The system as defined in claim 5, wherein the magnetic component includes a plurality of permanent magnets positioned in a multipole arrangement. 7. The system as defined in claim 6, wherein the needle is included in an insertion tool configured to assist in inserting a catheter into the body of the patient. 8. The system as defined in claim 7, wherein the insertion tool is disposed in a tray that is removably positionable on the magnetizer. 9. The system as defined in claim 8, wherein the orientation key system includes a first key feature included with the magnetizer and a second key feature included with the tray. 10. The system as defined in claim 9, wherein the first key feature includes a cavity defined in the body of the magnetizer and wherein the second key feature includes a protrusion defined on the tray. 11. The system as defined in claim 9, wherein the first key feature includes first and second posts on the body of the magnetizer and wherein the second key feature includes first and second holes defined in the tray. 12. The system as defined in claim 11, wherein the first and second posts are positioned in an offset configuration on the body of the magnetizer and the first and second holes in the tray are offset so as to respectively receive the first and second posts. 13. The system as described in claim 12, wherein the orientation key system is configured to inhibit placement of the tray on the magnetizer in an erroneous orientation. 14. The system as described in claim 13, wherein the first post defines a diameter that is different from a diameter defined by the second post, and wherein the first hole is sized to receive the first post and the second hole is sized to receive the second post. 15. The system as described in claim 14, wherein the orientation key system is configured to limit placement and removal of the tray on the magnetizer in a direction substantially orthogonal to a longitudinal length of a magnetic component of the magnetizer. 16. A magnetizer configured to magnetize a needle associated with a medical device, the magnetizer comprising: a body; a magnetic component included with the body; and an orientation key system including: a first key feature included with the body of the magnetizer, the first key feature configured to cooperatively and removably mate with a second key feature included with one of the needle and the medical device so as to position the needle of the device in a desired orientation with respect to the magnetizing component. 17. The magnetizer as defined in claim 16, wherein the medical device and needle are disposed in a tray, the tray configured to removably join with the magnetizer. 18. The system as defined in claim 16, wherein the needle is included in an insertion tool configured to assist in inserting a catheter into the body of a patient. 19. The system as defined in claim 18, wherein the insertion tool is disposed in a tray that is removably positionable on the magnetizer. 20. The system as defined in claim 19, wherein the second key feature is included with the tray. 21. The system as defined in claim 20, wherein the first key feature includes a cavity defined in the body of the magnetizer and wherein the second key feature includes a protrusion defined on the tray. 22. The system as defined in claim 20, wherein the first key feature includes first and second posts on the body of the magnetizer and wherein the second key feature includes first and second holes defined in the tray. 23. The system as defined in claim 22, wherein the magnetizer further includes an RFID reader configured to read an RFID tag included with the medical device. 24. The magnetizer as defined in claim 23, wherein the RFID tag is included on the tray. 25. The system as defined in claim 24, wherein the first and second posts are positioned in an offset configuration on the body of the magnetizer and the first and second holes in the tray are offset so as to respectively receive the first and second posts. 26. The system as described in claim 25, wherein the orientation key system is configured to inhibit placement of the tray on the magnetizer in an erroneous orientation. 27. The system as described in claim 26, wherein the first post defines a diameter that is different from a diameter defined by the second post, and wherein the first hole is sized to receive the first post and the second hole is sized to receive the second post. 28. The system as defined in claim 27, wherein the magnetic component includes a plurality of permanent magnets positioned in a multipole arrangement. 29. A method for magnetizing a needle of a medical device, the method comprising: aligning a second key feature included with the medical device with a first key feature included with a magnetizer; mating the first key feature with the second key feature so as to magnetize the needle of the medical device; and separating the first and second key features after magnetization of the needle. 30. The method as defined in claim 29, wherein the first key feature and the second key feature are configured to restrict a direction of the mating and the separating of the first and second key features to a predetermined direction. 31. The method as defined in claim 30, wherein the mating and the separating of the first and second key features in the predetermined direction prevents distortion of a magnetic field of the needle after magnetization of the needle. 32. The method as defined in claim 29, wherein the magnetizer includes a magnetic component and wherein the mating of the first key feature with the second key feature positions a distal tip of the needle substantially co-terminal with an end of the magnetic component. 33. The method as defined in claim 29, wherein the mating of the first key feature with the second key feature includes mating first and second posts included on a body of the magnetizer with first and second holes included on a tray, the tray including the medical device and the needle therein. 34. The method as defined in claim 29, wherein the medical device includes an insertion tool, the needle attached to the insertion tool, the insertion tool including a catheter that is insertable into a body of a patient. 35. A needle magnetizing system, comprising: a magnetizer including at least one permanent magnet; a needle location included with the magnetizer, the needle location configured to receive a needle; and a key feature associated with at least one of the needle and the magnetizer, the key feature configured to guide a user so as to place the needle in a predetermined position with respect to the at least one permanent magnet when the needle is received at the needle location. 36. The needle magnetizing system as defined in claim 35, wherein the key feature is included on a tray, the needle being removably included with the tray. 37. The needle magnetizing system as defined in claim 36, wherein the key feature of the tray includes two holes defined in the tray in an offset configuration, the two holes configured to receive therein two corresponding posts of the magnetizer when the tray is mated with the magnetizer. 38. The needle magnetizing system as defined in claim 35, wherein the key feature is included on a needle cover configured to removably cover the needle. 39. The needle magnetizing system as defined in claim 38, wherein the magnetizer is attached to the needle cover. 40. The needle magnetizing system as defined in claim 39, wherein the at least one permanent magnet of the magnetizer includes an annular magnet. 41. The needle magnetizing system as defined in claim 35, wherein the key feature is further configured to constrain placement and removal of the needle from the needle location to a predetermined placement and removal direction.
